q: Renewable energy sources pollute the environment and natural surroundings.
Energy demand forecasting is crucial for ensuring future economic growth and environmental security.
The global supply of energy is currently far greater than the actual demand.
Because the population and new technology are always expanding, demand for energy is expected to increase year by year.
The energy crisis is a situation in which a nation suffers from a disruption of energy supplies connected by increasing energy prices.
Non-renewable resources of energy will last for an extremely long time and can be regained easily once depleted.
Renewable energy has huge potential to provide a solution to the energy crisis and is a key factor for future food and economic security.
The rate of formation of fossil fuels is estimated to be about 4 billion kW.
The rate of consumption of fossil fuel is of a completely different order of magnitude compared to the rate of their formation.
In our daily life, the most common forms of energy we use are heat, light, and electricity.
A non-renewable resource is a natural substance that can be fully regenerated in a short period of time.
Renewable energy sources are continuously replenished by natural processes.
Renewable energy sources can be generated at a constant rate for their use over and over again.
Renewable energy sources can be produced or generated at a rate greater than or equal to the rate of their consumption.
It takes a relatively short time, often only a few hundred years, for non-renewable energy resources to be replenished.
Carbon is the main constituent of fossil fuels like coal, oil, and natural gas.
A large amount of electrical energy is directly stored inside raw fossil fuels.
Fossil fuels are excellent sources of energy for our transportation needs.
The fossil fuel industry extracts crude oil from the ground and converts it to gasoline.
Fossil fuel liquids are refined into petrochemical products that serve as ingredients for manufacturing plastics and polyurethane.
Coal is formed in a process called coalification.
Coal is a perfectly uniform substance whose chemical composition remains completely identical from deposit to deposit.
Coal is made of decomposed plant matter in conditions of high temperature and pressure.
Natural gas takes nearly as long to form as coal and oil deposits do.
Methane is a common gas found in swamps.
Methane is also a natural by-product of the digestive system of animals.
Nuclear energy is classified as a renewable energy source because uranium fuels are easily replenished during operation.
Uranium fuels used in nuclear energy are consumed in a nuclear fission reaction.
Vessels driven by nuclear energy must frequently stop to refuel during short-range voyages.
Radioisotopes obtained as by-products in nuclear reactions are actively used in medicine, agriculture, and research.
The major types of renewable energy sources consist of solar, wind, hydropower, ocean, geothermal, and biomass.
Earth's atmosphere and clouds absorb or scatter as much as 90% of all incoming sunlight.
The sunlight that reaches the ground consists of visible light, infrared, ultraviolet, and other forms of electromagnetic radiation.
Above the earth's atmosphere, solar radiation has an intensity known as the solar constant.
At the earth's surface, solar radiation has an intensity of approximately 1000 $\text{W m}^{-2}$ on a clear day at solar noon in the summer.
Photovoltaic solar cell, solar heating, and concentrated solar power (CSP) are the three main ways to harness solar energy.
The phenomenon of converting light energy directly into electrical energy is called the photovoltaic effect.
When semiconductor materials are exposed to light, photons cause a significant number of free protons to build up in the crystal.
A solar cell is basically a p-n junction diode.
When light is incident on a photovoltaic cell, light photons can easily reach the p-n junction.
Light energy in the form of photons supplies sufficient energy to a p-n junction to create a number of electron-hole pairs.
Individual solar cells are too delicate to ever be combined into modules or solar panels.
Achieving perfect perpendicular sun rays is the optimal orientation for solar panels.
Power conversion efficiency of a solar panel is the ratio of the electrical power delivered by the panel to the solar power received by it.
Solar heating systems play no role whatsoever in reducing the impacts of global warming.
Flat plate collectors are identified as the most common type of solar collector.
Flat plate collectors have iron pipes attached to a plastic absorber plate inside an uninsulated container.
Concentrated solar power (CSP) systems generate solar power by using mirrors or lenses.
CSP plants consist of two parts: one that collects solar energy and converts it to heat, and another that converts the heat energy to electricity.
Wind energy is generated or produced by harnessing the power of the wind using a wind turbine to generate electricity.
Harnessing the wind is highly independent of weather and location.
Windmills convert wind energy directly into electricity.
A wind turbine converts wind energy into electricity.
Wind farms consist of many individual wind turbines connected to the electric power transmission network.
Wind energy is the potential energy of air in motion.
The wind power output is proportional to the dimensions of the rotor and to the cube of the wind speed.
Theoretically, when wind speed doubles, wind power potential increases by a factor of four.
Wind turbines are mounted on a tower to capture the most energy by taking advantage of faster and less turbulent wind.
Usually, four or five blades are mounted on a shaft to form a rotor.
Hydropower is one of the oldest and largest sources of renewable energy.
The source of power in a hydroelectric plant is the temperature of water.
Hydroelectric power costs less than most of other sources of energy.
Harnessing water power can never have a devastating effect on the local environment and habitation areas.
The three types of hydropower facilities are impoundment, diversion, and pumped storage.
An impoundment facility typically uses a dam to store river water in a reservoir.
The difference in water elevations between the higher and lower levels in an impoundment system is known as the head.
A penstock is an open canal that channels water to the turbine.
In a hydroelectric plant, transformers convert the alternating voltage of generators to a higher voltage suitable for long-distance transmission.
A diversion facility always requires the use of a large dam.
A pumped storage hydropower facility is able to store electricity generated by other power sources.
During periods of high electrical demand, a PSH facility pumps water from the lower reservoir to the upper reservoir.
In the available power formula $P = \eta \rho \dot{V} g h$, the variable $h$ is the difference in height between the outlet and inlet.
A water turbine converts the mechanical energy of falling water into kinetic energy.
Tidal energy is produced due to the gravitational pull of mainly the moon on the spinning earth.
A tidal barrage is placed across a wide and open deep ocean channel.
Turbines anchored to the ocean floor or suspended from a buoy can be used to generate power from ocean currents.
The waves near the seashore are generated by strong winds blowing across the sea.
Most wave energy systems capture energy on the surface of waves or use pressure differences just below the surface.
Ocean thermal energy conversion plants can operate if the temperature difference between surface water and deep water up to $2\text{ km}$ is $5\text{ K}$ or more.
The boiling point of ammonia used in ocean thermal energy conversion plants is approximately $-33.34^\circ\text{C}$.
Efficient commercial exploitation of energy potential from the sea is currently very easy.
Geothermal energy is taken from the Earth's core.
Magma is extremely hot liquid and semi-liquid rock located under Earth's surface.
Geothermal resources are available uniformly in all regions of the world regardless of underground rocks.
Biomass is renewable organic material that comes from plants and animals.
Biomass contains stored electrical energy.
Biomass sources include wood, agricultural crops, biogenic materials in municipal solid waste, animal manure, and human sewage.
Direct combustion is the least common method for converting biomass to useful energy.
Thermochemical conversion of biomass includes pyrolysis and gasification.
Biomass gasification produces synthesis gas, which is rich in carbon monoxide and hydrogen.
The chemical conversion process known as transesterification converts vegetable oils into biodiesel.
Biological conversion of biomass includes fermentation to convert biomass into ethanol.
Anaerobic digestion produces synthesis gas at sewage treatment plants.
Properly treated renewable natural gas has different uses compared to fossil fuel natural gas.
Energy harnessing and utilization cause an immense amount of environmental damage.
The main greenhouse gases include carbon dioxide, methane, water vapour, oxides of nitrogen, and chlorofluorocarbons.
Chlorofluorocarbons protect the earth's ozone layer from ultraviolet rays.
Acid rains are caused mainly by the release of sulphur dioxide and oxides of nitrogen when fossil fuels burn.
Carbon dioxide alone has a very high ability to generate true acid rain because carbonic acid is a strong acid.
Acidified clouds always release acid rain immediately above the source of pollution without travelling.
Because carbonic acid is a relatively weak acid, the ability of carbon dioxide alone to generate true acid rain is very limited.
Acidified clouds always release their acid rain immediately near the original source of the air pollution.
The oil industry adds lead (Pb) to petrol (gasoline) primarily to help engines run more smoothly.
The World Health Organisation has established that smoke from the combustion of leaded fuels in vehicles causes cancer and high blood pressure in children.
The leakage of fuel oil from storage vessels, oil tankers, pipelines and tanker trucks is currently unavoidable.
Gas leaks and explosions sometimes accompany the harnessing and utilization of fossil fuels especially in the coal mines and storage plants.
Excavated areas that have been strip mined for coal but are not filled and revegetated cause air pollution as surface water runoff.
Photovoltaics is now a proven technology which is inherently safe, as opposed to some dangerous electricity generating technologies.
Over its estimated life, a photovoltaic module will produce exactly the same amount of electricity as was used in its production.
Photovoltaic systems make loud noises but cause no pollution while in operation.
PV cell technologies have relatively lower environmental risks compared to other types of electric sources.
Chemicals used in PV cells in the manufacturing facility, the installation site, and the disposal or recycling facility cannot be released to surface water and groundwater.
A wind farm occupies less land area per kilowatt hour (kWh) of electricity generated than any other energy conversion system.
Greenhouse gas emissions and air pollution produced by the construction of a wind farm are considered very tiny and declining.
Modern turbine designs which locate the blades downwind instead of upwind have significantly reduced the level of infrasound.
Wind turbines may create shadow flicker on residences when the sun passes behind the turbine.
Hydropower operations have massive and direct air quality impacts on the surrounding environment.
Construction and operation of hydropower dams can significantly affect natural river systems as well as fish and wildlife populations.
In flat basins, large amounts of submerged plant life caused by large dams decay aerobically.
Water in dams is hotter than the normal river water, meaning the dams serve as a heat sink.
Geothermal power is considered a relatively benign, or less harmful, source of energy.
Electricity generation from geothermal resources involves much higher greenhouse gas emission rates than that from fossil fuels.
Biomass power plants share some similarities with fossil fuel power plants because both involve the combustion of a feedstock to generate electricity.
Unlike coal and natural gas-fired power plants, biomass power plants do not require water for cooling.
Global warming emissions from the sourcing of biomass feedstock are identical regardless of the type of biomass used.
The energy transfer sequence for fossil fuels is: chemical energy $\rightarrow$ thermal energy $\rightarrow$ kinetic energy $\rightarrow$ electrical energy.
A non-renewable resource is a natural substance that is continuously replenished by natural processes.
Conversion of light energy into electrical energy is based on a phenomenon called the photovoltaic effect.
Geothermal energy is a type of renewable energy taken from the internal heat of the earth, or the Earth's core.
Acid rain is caused by a chemical reaction that begins when compounds like sulfur dioxide and nitrogen oxides are released into the air.
